title: Fix viewport fill on mobile and tablets

analysis.root_cause: renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale) conflates render buffer resolution with layout sizing. On low-tier devices (resolutionScale=0.5), the effective pixel ratio drops, and Three.js uses this ratio when computing the canvas backing store via setSize(). Additionally, CSS uses 100vh which on iOS Safari includes the address bar area, causing the canvas to extend beyond the visible viewport. No orientation change handling or safe-area-inset support exists.
analysis.key_insight: resolutionScale must only affect the render buffer pixel ratio, never the CSS layout dimensions. Canvas CSS size must always equal the visible viewport. On mobile, 100vh is unreliable — use dvh units or visualViewport API. UI buttons need safe-area-inset padding for notched devices.

steps.action: Refactor scene.ts — separate CSS sizing from render buffer scaling
steps.details: Change initScene() so that setSize() always uses window.innerWidth × window.innerHeight for CSS dimensions (the third parameter updateStyle=true is default). Set pixel ratio as Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale to scale only the backing buffer. Critically, call setSize() AFTER setPixelRatio() so Three.js computes the correct backing store from the full CSS dimensions. Add an explicit canvas style override after setSize to ensure CSS width/height are never affected by pixel ratio: renderer.domElement.style.width = '100%' and renderer.domElement.style.height = '100%'.
changes: Keep: renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale)
Keep: renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
Add: renderer.domElement.style.width = '100%'
Add: renderer.domElement.style.height = '100%'
This ensures CSS size always fills the #app container regardless of pixel ratio

steps.action: Refactor resize.ts — use visualViewport API with fallback, handle orientation
steps.details: Replace raw window.innerWidth/innerHeight with a helper that reads window.visualViewport (width/height) when available, falling back to window.innerWidth/innerHeight. Listen to both 'resize' on window AND 'resize' on window.visualViewport (if available) to catch iOS Safari viewport changes from address bar show/hide. Also listen to 'orientationchange' for legacy mobile browsers. After each resize, force canvas CSS to 100% of container to prevent layout drift.
changes: Add getViewportSize() helper: returns { width: visualViewport?.width ?? innerWidth, height: visualViewport?.height ?? innerHeight }
Update onResize to use getViewportSize() for setSize() calls
Maintain pixel ratio formula: Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) * scale
Add renderer.domElement.style.width = '100%' and height = '100%' after setSize
Add visualViewport?.addEventListener('resize', onResize) listener
Add window.addEventListener('orientationchange', onResize) listener
Return cleanup function that removes all three listeners

steps.action: Fix CSS — use dvh units with vh fallback, add safe-area-inset support
steps.details: Replace 100vh with 100dvh (dynamic viewport height) which excludes mobile browser chrome. Add vh fallback for older browsers. Add safe-area-inset padding to UI buttons so they aren't clipped behind notches or home indicators on modern phones.
changes: html, body: height: 100vh; height: 100dvh; (fallback then override)
html, body: width: 100vw; width: 100dvw;
#app: width: 100%; height: 100%;
canvas: display: block; width: 100% !important; height: 100% !important; (prevent Three.js inline style override)
.eavi-mute-btn: bottom: max(1.5rem, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 0.75rem); left: max(1.5rem, env(safe-area-inset-left, 0px) + 0.75rem);
.eavi-info-btn: bottom: max(1.5rem, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 0.75rem); right: max(1.5rem, env(safe-area-inset-right, 0px) + 0.75rem);

steps.details: Update the viewport meta tag to include viewport-fit=cover, which allows the page to render into the safe area on notched devices (required for env(safe-area-inset-*) to work).
changes: Change: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
To: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover">

steps.action: Update main.ts — ensure resize handler reattachment uses new API
steps.details: The two-phase initialization (immediate scene + deferred quality) already reattaches the resize handler. Ensure the reattachment at lines 57-60 correctly passes resolutionScale and that the explicit canvas style overrides are applied after re-initialization.
changes: After renderer.setSize() at line 57, add canvas style overrides
Ensure cleanupResize() properly removes all listeners (resize, visualViewport, orientationchange)

steps.details: Update existing tests T-025-09 through T-025-11 to verify that canvas CSS dimensions remain at full viewport size regardless of resolutionScale, while backing store (canvas.width/height) scales with pixel ratio. Add new tests for orientation change handling and visualViewport integration.
new_tests: T-028-01: Canvas CSS size equals viewport dimensions regardless of resolutionScale (0.5, 0.75, 1.0)
T-028-02: Canvas backing store (canvas.width) equals viewport width × effectivePixelRatio
T-028-03: Orientation change event triggers resize and updates dimensions
T-028-04: visualViewport resize event triggers canvas resize when API available
T-028-05: Resize handler cleanup removes all listeners (resize, orientationchange, visualViewport)

steps.action: Update tests — resize.test.ts for new listener behavior
steps.details: Update T-025-18 and T-025-19 to verify CSS dimensions stay full-viewport while backing store scales. Add tests for orientationchange and visualViewport listeners.
new_tests: T-028-06: Resize handler maintains CSS size at 100% after resolutionScale application
T-028-07: Cleanup function removes orientationchange listener
T-028-08: Resize with visualViewport uses viewport dimensions, not window.innerWidth

steps.details: Run npm run build to ensure no compilation errors. Verify no console errors. Check that canvas element has CSS width/height of 100% and backing store scales independently.
verification: npm test passes with updated tests
Canvas fills viewport on simulated mobile viewports
Resolution scaling only affects backing store, not CSS dimensions

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Visual output fills the viewport on mobi: Steps 1-3: Canvas CSS forced to 100% of container, dvh units for mobile viewport, visualViewport API for iOS Safari
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Resolution scaling does not shrink the c: Steps 1-2: Canvas style.width/height set to 100% after setSize(), pixel ratio only affects backing store
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Orientation changes update sizing correc: Step 2: orientationchange listener + visualViewport resize listener in attachResizeHandler
acceptance_criteria_mapping.UI buttons remain reachable and not clip: Steps 3-4: safe-area-inset env() values on buttons, viewport-fit=cover meta tag

risks.risk: canvas width/height 100% !important in CSS may conflict with Three.js internal setSize behavior
risks.mitigation: Test that Three.js still correctly computes backing store when CSS dimensions are overridden. If conflict exists, apply style overrides via JS after each setSize() call instead of CSS !important.

risks.risk: visualViewport API not available in test environment (jsdom)
risks.mitigation: Mock window.visualViewport in tests with configurable width/height and addEventListener stub

risks.risk: dvh units not supported in older browsers
risks.mitigation: CSS fallback chain: height: 100vh first, then height: 100dvh override. Older browsers ignore dvh line.
